setup.context: Predicting what a dancing robot will do

root.narration: A dancing robot has these instructions: Step 1) Wave left hand. Step 2) Wave right hand. Step 3) Jump up. Step 4) Bow. If you press the START button, what will the robot do FIRST?

choices.text: Do everything at the same time.

outcome.headline: Correct! Step 1 Goes First!
outcome.explanation: The robot always starts at Step 1 and goes in order. Wave left hand is Step 1, so that happens first! Reading code is like reading a recipe -- you start at the top and go down, one step at a time.
outcome.whatToRemember: Computers start at the first instruction and go down the list in order. To figure out what code does, read from the top!

keyTerms.definition: Reading through code step by step to figure out what it will do, just like following a recipe.

outcome.headline: Not Quite! Jump is Step 3!
outcome.explanation: Jump is in the list, but it is Step 3, not Step 1! The robot has to do Step 1 and Step 2 before it gets to Step 3. Computers always go in order from top to bottom.
outcome.whatToRemember: Always read from the top. The computer does Step 1 first, then Step 2, then Step 3, and so on.

outcome.headline: Nope! One Thing at a Time!
outcome.explanation: Computers do one step at a time, not everything at once! The robot cannot wave both hands AND jump AND bow all at the same moment. It follows the list: first this, then that, then the next thing.
outcome.whatToRemember: A computer does ONE step at a time, in order. It does not do everything at once!
